title: Bard Broker Link Frames
description: Additive v1.1/v1.2 contract (ADR-0013, root features.md #59): JSON text frames on the agent's persistent outbound WebSocket to the Router (/v1/agent-link). The frozen protocol.schema.json envelopes (Request/Response/Error) ride inside these frames unchanged; the v1 HTTP surfaces are untouched. Handshake: agent sends Hello (its agentId + a JWT the Router's TokenVerifier accepts); Router replies HelloOk, or closes 1008 on a bad hello/token. Dispatch: Router sends InferRequest with a unique frameId; the agent replies InferResponse or InferError carrying the SAME frameId (correlation key — concurrent frames may interleave). One link per agentId; a newer connection replaces the older one (Router closes it, code 1012). v1.2 (slice 2 — single front door): in broker mode the agent registers and heartbeats OVER THE LINK instead of POSTing the now-private Registry directly. After hello_ok the agent sends a Register frame; on the heartbeat interval it sends Heartbeat frames (identical body — a heartbeat IS a /register refresh, keeping lastSeen/TTL semantics unchanged). The Router relays both to the Registry's existing /register on the agent's behalf, BINDING THE REGISTRATION TO THE agentId THE LINK AUTHENTICATED AS in its hello — any agentId carried in a register/heartbeat frame is ignored (security: register-over-link cannot impersonate another agent; see bug #54). The advertisedAddress in these frames is the link itself, not a dialable host: the Router reaches the agent down the link, never by dialing the address. Agents SHOULD omit advertisedAddress (the Router then synthesizes the sentinel "broker://<agentId>") or set it to that sentinel explicitly; placement/pool key off powerProfile/capabilities/status, never the address, so the sentinel is inert. Router acks with Registered (best-effort; the agent does not block on it).

FrameId.description: Router-generated correlation id, unique per in-flight dispatch on a link.

authToken.description: JWT verified by the Router's TokenVerifier (same seam as /v1/message).

HelloFrame.description: agent -> router, first frame after connect.

HelloOkFrame.description: router -> agent, handshake acknowledgement; the link is live.

InferRequestFrame.description: router -> agent: serve this through the same engine as POST /infer.

InferResponseFrame.description: agent -> router: successful completion for the correlated frameId.

InferErrorFrame.description: agent -> router: the correlated dispatch failed (unauthorized / unsupported_type / inference_failed / bad_request). The Router maps any InferError to 502 agent_unavailable retry:true — parity with the HTTP-dial path.

RegistrationBody.description: Shared body of Register/Heartbeat frames (slice 2). Deliberately carries NO agentId — the Router binds the registration to the agentId the link authenticated as in its hello (security: a register/heartbeat frame can never claim another agent's identity; see bug #54). The fields mirror registry.openapi.yaml AgentRegistration minus agentId/address-as-dialable: advertisedAddress is the broker-link sentinel ("broker://<agentId>"), defaulted by the Router when omitted.

advertisedAddress.description: SHOULD be omitted (Router synthesizes "broker://<agentId>") or set to that sentinel. NOT a dialable host: the Router reaches the agent down the link. Placement/pool never dial this.

powerProfile.description: Opaque passthrough — the Router forwards it verbatim to /register, where the Registry validates it against power-profile.schema.yaml (same as the HTTP path). Kept loose here so the link contract does not duplicate the frozen power-profile schema.

RegisterFrame.description: agent -> router (slice 2): first registration over the link, sent right after hello_ok. Router relays to the Registry's /register bound to the link's authenticated agentId.

HeartbeatFrame.description: agent -> router (slice 2): periodic liveness refresh, identical body to Register. A heartbeat frame IS a /register refresh — same lastSeen/TTL semantics as the HTTP heartbeat (feature #54). If the link drops, the agent simply goes stale by the normal TTL; no second liveness path.

RegisteredFrame.description: router -> agent (slice 2): best-effort ack that a Register/Heartbeat was relayed to the Registry. The agent does not block on it.
